name: Potee Lorraine
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds boneless pork shoulder, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
3 cans cannellini beans (white kidney beans), rinsed and drained (15-ounce)
1 pound green beans, trimmed and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bacon in a heavy 8-quart Dutch oven over high heat until fat is rendered. This should take about 4 minutes.
2. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the cooked bacon to a medium bowl. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of the rendered fat and set it aside.
3. Heat the reserved fat in the pot over high heat.
4. Working in batches, add pork cubes, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and brown lightly on all sides. Transfer each batch to the bowl containing the bacon.
5. Add onions to the pot. Reduce heat to medium and cook until tender, which should take about 8 minutes.
6. Return the pork and bacon to the pot.
7. Add broth, wine, and bay leaves. Cover and simmer for 1 hour.
8. Cut cabbage, carrots, turnips, and potatoes into pieces that are 3/4- to 1-inch in size.
9. Heat the reserved bacon fat in a heavy large skillet over medium-high heat.
10. Add cabbage and cook until wilted, stirring often, which should take about 5 minutes.
11. Add carrots, turnips, and potatoes and stir for another 5 minutes.
12. Add the vegetables to the pork. Cover and simmer until the pork is tender, which should take about 45 minutes.
13. Add cannellini beans to the stew. Simmer uncovered until the stew thickens, stirring occasionally, which should take about 15 minutes.
14. Add ground cloves. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
- This dish can be prepared up to one day ahead. After preparing, cover and refrigerate. To reheat, warm over medium heat, stirring frequently.
15. Cook green beans in a large pot of boiling salted water until crisp-tender, which should take about 5 minutes. Drain and mix into the stew.
16. Ladle the stew into shallow bowls and serve.
